title: Be An Instrument in the Hands of Lord | Bhagavad Gita 11.31 - 11.37 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, we are covering text number 31 to 37 today from the 11th chapter of Bhagavad Gita as it is.
Based on teachings of Srila Prabhupada.
We will chant these words together. Please repeat after me. We will go word by word first.
Aakyahi meko bhavan ugra rupo. Namostu te devavara prasida. Vignatum ichchami bhavantamadyam. Nahi prajnami tavapravityam.
Translation.
O Lord of Lords, so fierce of form, please tell me who you are. I offer my obeisances unto you. Please be gracious to me. I do not know what your mission is and I desire to hear of it.
These are the words spoken by two.
On seeing.
Vishwaroopa or the universal form.
So, he is saying, O Lord of Lords, so fierce of form, please tell me who you are.
What he is seeing right in front of him is the universal form which is gigantic with all the devatas and all the people gushing into the mouths.
Very fearsome form.
I offer my obeisances unto you. Please be gracious on me.
I do not know what your mission is and I desire to hear of it.
I do not know what your mission is.
What this form is all about.
Why have you given me this vision?
I desire to hear of it.
So, now, when we all come here on Sunday, we all assemble, or for that matter, any activity we do, there is a purpose.
we don't do anything whimsically even for a company you know generally the directors or
the promoters sit together and then they do some bit of thinking on what is the mission
what's the vision what's the values and if the company is aligned to the mission and vision then
they say that it is in alignment otherwise mission is something else doing something
else so Lord also has a mission this purpose this world is created for a purpose now Arjuna
is inquisitive to ask I am interested to know what your mission is what is all this about
you
text 32 the blessed Lord said time I am destroyer of the worlds and I have come to engage all people
with the exception of you the Pandavas all the soldiers here on both sides will be slain
who developed or invented the atomic bomb anyone as an answer atomic bomb atom bomb
Robert Oppenheimer okay so he quoted this verse when the first time the bomb was actually
executed time I am the destroyer of everything so Lord has a very important energy which is
called kala kala means color doesn't mean black kala means time and nature of time is it devours
devours means now you see anything in this world you take a new car it's brand new lustros very very
good to look at time devours after 10 years after 15 years after 20 years after 25 years a time comes
you have to junk it
Buildings
Very Nice
nicely built, 30 years, 40 years, 50 years, they demolish. Time I am, that Kala is the
energy of the Lord. Now, every one of us have to surrender to the Lord, knowingly, unknowingly,
willingly or unwillingly. So, one time, a person said, why I have to surrender? I don't
surrender to God. I don't believe in God. Why I should surrender? Why Krishna is demanding
surrender to me? So, Prabhupada said, in fact, you have no choice. When old age comes, you
have to surrender.
You cannot say, I will not surrender to old age. I will not become old. When diseases
come, you have to surrender. When death comes, you have to surrender.
Mrityu sarva haras chaham. Krishna is saying Mrityu. Mrityu means death, all devouring
death. Sarva haras means it devours everything. Chaham, I am that. Now, I will explain as
we move forward.
Sometimes, it may appear that, you know, this is instilling fear in us. The Lord is
saying, I am the death. I am fear personified. Surrender to me, otherwise you will be killed.
Let's understand the context. So, everyone has to surrender. Now, what a devotee does
is, he willingly surrenders. He accepts the supremacy of the Lord. Accepts that, yes,
we are subordinates.
Say to the Lord, yes, you are the master. Yes, you are the controller. Yes, we are controlled.
Yes, we are not all powerful. We have some powers. We have some abilities. We can control
few things. But you are the all powerful. You are the supreme controller. So, that way,
with this knowledge.
And natural consequences, the devotee becomes humble. And his dependency on the Lord increases.
He depends on the Lord with the understanding that, if the Lord protects, I am safe. Not
because I have lot of money. Not because I have lot of strength. If I have the mercy
and grace of the Lord, I am safe. If I don't have the mercy of the Lord, in spite of having
everything, I am not safe. What happens is, this is the meaning of illusion or Maya.
When we have money, bank balance, near and dear ones, well-wishers around us, there is
a sense of, I am safe. Security. The true security is in understanding that Lord is
with me. That's why a devotee actually is fearless.
less. He knows Lord is with me, therefore I am safe, not because of my money. And that's
the reason why in spite of ups and downs, money is there, not there. The devotee has
an equanimity, steadiness, because he knows Lord will surely protect me. We all are looking
for stability, security, sense of steadiness, and we are trying to secure ourselves through
various means. Bhagavatam says we all have different means to secure ourselves. We all
have, we build just like a king to secure ourselves, or for that matter country to secure
ourselves, we build the armed forces, we buy weaponry, we buy ammunitions. Now in newspaper
you are hearing,
India is spending billions of dollars to secure ourselves. So Bhagavatam says we all have
a sainiya or army around us, which we build around us. What is that army consisting of?
Let's contribute. That army is built of what? Which gives us a sense of security. That's
the first thing. First is Deha. Deha means body. Today, for good reason, nothing wrong
about it, people are very health conscious. One hour, two hours in the gym. Why? If I
have a good, strong, healthy body, diseases won't attack me. I am safe. Isn't it? That's
the first thing.
Now, what is the consciousness? Why are We doing what we are doing? If I have a strong
and stout body, I can ward off diseases. I will not become old easily. I can, to some
extent, defer aging. So it gives me a sense of security. I don't have any ailment. I am
sixty five. I am healthy, by the Lord's grace. We say it – Lord's grace. So janpayya ileesi,
we say Lord's grace.
So, we pursue health. It gives me security. I am good health, good body, strong, stout, healthy.
Deha, then Aapta. Aapta means we build network around us of friends, relatives, acquaintances, so that I am safe.
They will help me. Sometimes we help others also because today I am helping, tomorrow when I need, they will be there to help me.
Otherwise, if I don't help me, sometimes even in our so-called selfless action, there is a selfish motive. Yes or no?
Even our so-called selfless action, even helping others, there is a selfish motive.
And the background could be my own security.
Deha, Aapta, Kalat Radishu. Kalat Radishu.
Kalat means my wife, my family, my children, the near and dear ones.
So, Bhagavatam says, Atma Saineshu Asat Swapi.
This Atma Sainya, which we build around us for our security, it's Asat.
Asat means it is not permanent, it is not enduring, it will not really protect us.
Asat Swapi.
Tesham Pramatta Nidhanam Pashyana Peena Pashyati.
Tesham, seeing all that, Pramatta, Pramatta means maddened, intoxicated.
Tesham Pramatta Nidhanam, Nidhanam means eventual death.
Nidhan, Nidhan means to death.
Tesham Pramatta, Pramatta means when we are in illusion with that.
Tesham Pramatta, Nidhanam, Pashyana, Pashyana means to see.
Pashyati, Pashyana, Pashyana Peena Pashyati.
Although we know that death is inevitable, but we are not able to see.

Because I have all this, because I am now secure, I have near and dear ones, I have money, I have bank balance, I have doctors around me, I have an insurance policy, I have LIC, I have all the people who will support me.
Tesham Pramatta Nidhanam Pashyati.
Tesham Pramatta Nidhanam, Nidhanam, Pashyana Peena Pashyati are not able to see.
And that's why that real dependence on the Lord does not awaken.
Because faith is in our money, faith is in our near and dear ones, faith is that they will protect me.
And whole life we are building that Saini around us, we are investing on that Saini.
I am secure now.
Now Bhagavatam says you may have the best of doctors, the best of medicines, but if the Lord does not protect, the medicines will not be able to protect you.
And many times in our lives, we see real life examples. Isn't it? Life teaches us.
We have everything, the best of doctors, the best of medicines, the best of money to support us, but we are helpless.
Right in front of eyes, somebody near and dear ones is suffering, suffering, suffering and finally dies.
So, a devotee, understanding that he, no doubt, as a matter of duty, it's not that money is not important, it's not that near and dear ones are not important,
but he simultaneously understands that my real security is the Lord and he builds that dependency.
Arjuna was not in favor of fight, as we all know.
The first chapter he says, Krishna,
what's the use and all that. Now, Lord is showing that there is a higher plan.
They all are getting devoured. All the army who are gathered in the battlefield,
except for the Pandavas, they all will be slain.
Therefore, get up and prepare to fight. After conquering your enemies, you will enjoy a flourishing kingdom.
They are already put to death by my arrangement.
And you, O Savyasachin, can be,
but an instrument in the fight.
So, in this world, no doubt we are expected to do our duties, but there is a higher plan of the Lord.
It is said that for any action, there are five factors of action.
We will cover that separately.
Endeavor is one of the five factors.
We think that endeavor is everything.
And that's why many times we get dejected or disappointed.
I worked so hard, I did so much, but why I didn't get the result?
That's why many, many places in the Gita, Krishna says, you have the right to do your duty.
Do not be attached to the results of your activities.
There are many factors which are detrimental or which determine the eventual outcome.
Endeavor is that which determines the outcome.
Endeavor is just one of them.
Which is the final and foremost, the ultimate cause.
Can anyone tell me?
According to Gita, Krishna speaks about that.
In five factors of action, what is the ultimate factor, the most important factor, the differentiator, what is that?
It's the Super Soul, the Lord.
So, the Lord has a higher plan.
So, the Lord has a higher plan.
So, the Lord has a higher plan.
In that higher plan, we have to align ourselves.
Let your will be done.
The more we are aligned, our endeavor should be, how by my actions I can please the Lord.
How can I be in alignment with the desires of the Lord?
That should be our endeavor.
Many times our endeavor is, we are greedy for results.
And that's why we get disappointed, disheartened, dejected.
Endeavor, the ideal scenario, it's difficult.
That is part of purification.
A devotee endeavors, although he is self-satisfied, to please the Lord.
To satisfy the Lord.
The Lord has a mission in this world.
The world is created for a purpose.
So, he is also active, but to satisfy the Lord.
So, therefore, here Krishna is telling Arjuna, just become a nimitta.
Nimitta means, be a nimitta, instrument in the hands of the Lord.
Nimitra matra. Matra means only, only an instrument.
Whole world is moving according to the plan of the Lord.
Now, what is the plan? Why is this world created?
Can anyone help me with an answer?
Why is this world created?
Lord has created this world. It has not come from nowhere.
Anything we see which is orderly, just like satellites moving around Earth,
somebody has created, somebody has put in the orbit for a purpose.
Why is this world created?
Yes.
Hare Krishna.
We wanted to enjoy, have a sense of gratification or be the Lord.
And forgetting our basic constitutional position as a server.
So, Lord has, because of his generosity, he gave us a chance to be the Lord.
The fourth ego that we developed.
So, he created this world so that we can for some time indulge,
but ultimately come to a realization that this is our fault.
Okay, good point.
Anybody else would like to compliment, add, subtract?
Yes, Anupji.
One of the pastimes of the Lord, because ultimately he is the ultimate enjoyer.
So, pastime of the Lord, why there is so much suffering in this pastime?
Lord is enjoying that we are sad, someone is dying.
Family members are all suffering.
Somebody is old.
He is bedridden, can't pass stools.
Can't part this thing.
Lord is enjoying all this.
Why has he created such a world where there is happiness but also sadness?
If the Lord is all powerful, he could have created a world which is all bliss.
We are blessed.
We are his children.
Will a father not like his child to be always happy?
So, why has he created a world where there are tsunamis, earthquakes, famine, droughts, floods?
There is so much destruction.
So many people die.
Why?
He is enjoying it.
Or it could be that he is all-controller, he is all-powerful, he is a well-wisher,
but he is out of his control now.
Right?
Yes.
Sometimes it happens that things go out of your control.
You want to control your children.
What do you do now?
They are all ruined.
You don't listen to me now.
Okay, leave it now.
They are out of my control.
So, even God has lost control.
Why the Lord has created this world where there is so much of pain, suffering, good, bad?
Yes.
Why?
What is the purpose?
Yes.
So, it's a platform where we can revive our original lost consciousness.
Okay, good.
Yes, Anupji.
Lord could have given us a free will which is completely aligned to Him.
If God controls everything, everything is moving as per the higher arrangement of the Lord,
He could have arranged that nobody does anything wrong.
The terrorists don't get an idea to kill someone.
There are no murderers, there are no rapists, there are no serial killers, there are no
decoys, there is no thievery.
Everything is in order because it is controlled by the Lord.
But we see there are a lot of things going wrong.
Why?
Very nice.
Very nice.
Thank you.
Good.
So, Krishna in the Gita says, bahunam janmanam ante gyanvan.
Gyanvan means one who is in knowledge.
Gyanvan mam prapadyante vasudevam sarvam iti sa mahatma durlabha.
We have come in this material world where we have lost the dependency on the Lord.
We have lost the dependency on the Lord, lost the remembrance of the Lord, lost the understanding
that Lord is the controller, Lord is the enjoyer, Lord is the master.
We want to be the Lord.
We have our own desires completely not aligned to the desires of the Lord and we want to
be happy.
Just like it looks good for a person to be free and not follow any of these traffic laws.
Why do I have to stop when the red light comes?
Why do I have to pay taxes?
Why do I have to pay water bill?
It's good when I am free.
Nobody, there is no laws binding me.
But if I am not law abiding, if suppose I don't stop at the red light, the traffic police
will harass me, will penalize me till I become, till I become, till I become law abiding.
So, Lord has given us a free will.
A choice.
Yatha ichchasi tatha karu.
Now you do what you want.
He does not force us to obey Him.
He does not force us to worship Him.
He does not force us to desire the way He wants us to desire.
He does not force.
He has come and spoken the Gita.
He has sent His representatives.
They are telling us to act in this way, to surrender to Him, to remember Him.
The choice is ours.
So, so long as we violate the laws knowingly or unknowingly, just like you violate a law,
the police will penalize you.
In material laws, you can still go scot free if you are too smart.
But in this laws, we cannot go scot free.
On one side we want to be happy, unknowingly, unintentionally or sometimes intentionally,
we are breaking the laws.
For our selfish enjoyment.
I don't care.
I like eating it, I will eat.
If you don't like it, don't eat it.
And then, the nature penalizes.
Prakriti kriyamane gunai karmani sarvasya.
This Prakriti is acting under the direction of
Maya adhyakshena prakriti suyatesa achara acharam.
Under my direction, Prakriti is acting.
It is not acting independently.
Tsunami is not happening on its own.
So, we violate the laws.
Just like government is not hell bent on punishing us.
Government has not created prison house ke mujhe maja aa raha hai.
But there are some people who will violate the laws.
So, the prison house is there.
There is a department to punish such people.
So that they mend their ways and become law abiding citizens.
So, bahu naam janma naam ante.
After many many lives of trial and error,
trying to be happy, independent of the law,
trying to secure ourselves,
trying to do everything independent of the law,
Gyanvaan, a person who is sufficiently experienced,
who has sufficiently realized,
maam prapadyante,
will surrender to me willingly.
And what will be his understanding?
Vasudevam sarvam iti sa mahatma.
That Atma is Mahanatma and Durlabha.
A very rare person.
A very rare person will come to that understanding.
And how?
After many many lifetimes.
So, why this world is created?
This Dukha is there.
We violate, we get a beating.
Which is also there in the outside.
You violate a traffic law, you get a beating.
You violate a state law, you get a beating.
May not be beating physically.
May be in some other time, penalty.
You violate the income tax law, they will penalize you.
So, this world is created for our purification.
So, this world is created for our purification.
We come to our senses.
Cosmic manifestation is a chance for the conditioned soul to go back to home, back to Godhead.
It's a chance.
The Blessed Lord said,
All the great warriors, Drona, Bhishma, Jayadratha, Karna are already destroyed.
All the great warriors, Drona, Bhishma, Jayadratha, Karna are already destroyed.
Simply fight and you will vanquish your enemies.
Everything is happening as per the higher plan of the Lord.
When we say, happening as per the higher plan of the Lord,
It is not that the Lord has planned that terrorist will attack.
The Lord has planned that I will murder someone.
The Lord is involved in terms of witness and sanction.

The plans of the Supreme Personality of Godhead are understood by His mercy. It's not easy to understand the plans of the Lord.
Why the Lord has sanctioned this? Just yesterday, believe me, I received a call from Deputy Municipal Commissioner of Thane, TMC.
He was involved with us for giving permission for the Akshaya Patra Kitchen.
So, after a long time, maybe about four months, just suddenly, yesterday I was travelling and I got a call.
He said, Swamiji, can I speak to you for two minutes? I said, yes, please.
So, he says, do you remember that I brought a lady with me to your kitchen?
And that lady is also part of the Krishna Consciousness Movement.
She goes to the mirror of the Lord.
She goes to the mirror of the Lord regularly.
So, Swamiji, yesterday I was whole day at her place.
Her only son, who was 32 years old, died of cancer.
And the time period between diagnosing the cancer and he expiring, it was only three months.
32 year old boy, just married.
Just married few years back, has two year old daughter.
So, he said, Swamiji, that Bahu, that wife of that man who died, she was inconsolable.
And I saw your devotees coming from Mira Road.
There was Kirtan and in the evening there was a Paath, Shoksaba and all that.
And today morning when I went,
there was serenity in that house.
There was peace.
I could have never imagined that.
This is what you all are doing a great thing.
And these are his words.
Keep it up.
Keep doing what you are doing.
Society requires this intervention.
Otherwise, people cannot handle this.
He says, what this lady...
Imagine the only son, 32 year old, three months back, diagnosed with cancer.
Today lost his life.
Has one daughter, two years old.
The lady is calm.
Lady is able to handle it.
It is only when we have...
No doubt, she is also crying.
It's not that she is, you know, unaffected.
But there is a higher understanding.
We are not completely rattled.
A sober person is not bewildered by such a change.
When we talk about all these things,
looks like we are being pessimist.
We are talking about all negatives.
Death, old age, disease.
We are talking about realities of life.
We can close our eyes but eventually every one of us have to face.
So better be prepared.
Better not close our lives and live in illusion that nothing is going to happen to me.
Death is a reality.
Old age is a reality.
Disease is a reality.
It will happen to all of us.
To our near and dear ones.
To whom we are so dearly attached.
It's going to happen to them.
Are we prepared for it?
Do we have the required knowledge to deal with it?
And when we are in touch with the reality, then we can handle it.
So now the plans of the Lord are difficult to know.
For him, he asked me this question.
No one should suffer like this.
But what God does, seems to be a very God conscious person.
So no one should suffer like this, God.
And then he said very interesting thing.
He said maybe, maybe the Lord has a higher plan.
Because that was part of his plan that this has to unfold.
He had given her that knowledge and prepared her to take that shock.
It is his realization.
The plans of the devotees are now, it's difficult to comprehend why the Lord, that lady whose son died.
She is a devotee chanting 16 rounds.
Now somebody can question why it happened to her.
She is a devotee.
Why the Lord did not protect?
You are chanting.
Finally, when the time of protection came, the Lord stepped back.
So it's difficult to comprehend.
But the Lord has a higher plan.
The plans of the devotees are as good as his plans.
One should follow such plans and be victorious in the struggle for existence.
So this existence is a struggle for existence.
Prabhupada gives an example that just like an Atlantic Ocean.
If suppose somebody, a ship capsizes in Atlantic Ocean.
It's a huge ocean.
Miles and miles, miles and miles of water.
You may be an expert swimmer.
Just by your ability, you cannot swim across.
Imagine.
It takes, flight takes 14 hours or 12 hours.
You know, if I cross that ocean.
Imagine in the middle of ocean, the ship capsizes.
You cannot swim across just by your own might.
Somebody has to come and pick you up.
So likewise, this is Samsara Sagara.
It's an ocean of material existence.
Punarapi janama, punarapi nama.
Somebody has to pick us up.
And that is what the Lord does.
That is what he does by sending his representatives.
To pick us up from this material existence.
We talked about last session A, B, C, D.
Let's recap.
What is A?
Association.
For us to get out of this material existence, we need sadhana.
To achieve the sadhya.
What is a sadhya?
The goal to purify our existence.
Awaken our dependence on the Lord and go back to him.
That is the sadhya, goal.
To achieve that goal, we need a process.
Sadhana.
And sadhana consists of A.
A is association.
Sat sang.
Asat sanga tyaga.
E vaishnava achara.
Achara means a behavior, conduct of a vaishnava, devotee is.
He asat sanga tyaga.
He discriminates, uses his intelligence to avoid wrong association.
Asat sanga tyaga.
Tyaga means to give up.
Every moment we are given a choice.
To choose.
What to eat, what not to eat.
Whom to speak to, whom to associate with, whom to not associate with.
We have to use our intelligence.
So A, association, helps us with what?
We talked about A handles some aspect of us.
B handles some aspect of us.
A handles what?
A satsanga.
Helps us dismantle our false ego.
Ahankara.
Now I am safe.
Now I am in control.
Association, satsanga brings us in touch with reality.
It dismantles illusion.
B.
B stands for books, scriptures, knowledge.
B helps us sharpen our spiritual intelligence.
Whereby we can discern, where we can make the right choices.
C stands for chanting.
Chanting helps us tame or purify our mind.
Mind which is chanchala.
Which is always jumping.
To fix the mind on the name of the Lord.
It's part of sadhana.
By doing that, eventually the mind gets fixed, tamed, purified.
And D stands for diet or deity.
When we have a deity, we have the diet.
The food offered to the Lord.
And that food offered to the Lord or worship of deity.
It purifies our senses.
Eyes can see the beautiful form of the Lord.
Ears can hear the glories of the Lord.
The kirtans of the Lord.
The glorification of the Lord.
We can use our tongue to taste the nice eatables which are offered to the Lord.
Our hands to clean the temple of the Lord.
The nose to smell the aroma.
The flowers which is offered to the Lord.
And thereby gradually the senses can get purified.
So we have the senses, we have the mind, we have the intelligence, we have the false ego and then us, the soul.
So A, B, C, D purifies these aspects of us.
Text 35
Sanjaya said to Dhritarashtra,
O King, after hearing these words from the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
Arjuna trembled fearfully, offered obeisances with folded hands and began falteringly to speak the follows.
He began to pray not as a friend but as a devotee in wonder.
Initially all the conversation of Arjuna and Krishna is like a friend talking to each other.
But when Arjuna realized that he is not an ordinary friend.
When the Lord showed his Vishwaroopa, you know all the devatas are there,
all the warriors are there, everyone is entering this thing in a fearsome form, huge, magnanimous form.
He realized it's not an ordinary person I am talking to.
He is not an ordinary person.
So it was more of a devotee in wonder, wonderstruck.
The opulence of the Lord, the mightiness of the Lord.
O Rishikesha, now this is the words of Arjuna.
The world becomes joyful upon hearing your name and thus everyone becomes attached to you.
Although the perfected beings offer you their respectful homage,
the demons are afraid and they flee here and there.
All this is rightly done.
Now devotees glorify the Lord because he knows whatever he does is for good of everyone.
Faith means we don't question why me, as a kyun kya.
Surrender.
The devotee is fixed.
